extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. The variable `sent` is set to true ONLY by transition t2 (send_attempt with guard `not opted_out`), which by its guard can only fire when opted_out is false, leaving the state opted_out=false, sent=true. Every other transition that touches sent sets it to false: t1 (optout: opted_out:=true, sent:=false), t3 (send_attempt when opted_out: sent:=false), and t4 (tick: sent:=false). The variable opted_out becomes true only via t1, and t1 simultaneously sets sent:=false; no transition ever sets opted_out back to false. Therefore, in any reachable state where opted_out=true, the last transition to set opted_out true (t1) also cleared sent, and while opted_out remains true the only enabled transitions are t3 (sent:=false), t4 (sent:=false), and t1 (sent:=false) — t2 is permanently disabled because its guard requires not opted_out. Hence sent can never be true while opted_out is true. The invariant is inductive: it holds initially (opted_out=false, vacuous) and is preserved by every transition.
